volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: The Women of Trachis (Section 1045 - Section 1190)

sections.label: Section 1045
sections.original: ἄνακτος , οἵαις οἷος ὢν ἐλαύνεται .
sections.translation: which our King suffers . What a man is here , and what misfortunes lash at him !

sections.label: Section 1046
sections.original: ὦ πολλὰ δὴ καὶ θερμὰ κοὐ λόγῳ κακὰ
sections.translation: Ah , many and hot and cruel not in name alone have been the labors of these hands , the burdens hoisted upon these shoulders ! And yet no toil ever laid on me by the bedfellow of Zeus or by the hateful Eurystheus was as harsh

sections.label: Section 1050
sections.original: οἷον τόδʼ ἡ δολῶπις Οἰνέως κόρη
sections.translation: as this thing which the daughter of Oeneus , fair and false , has fastened upon my back , this woven net of the Erinyes in which I perish ! Plastered to my sides , it has eaten away my inmost flesh and sucks the channels of my lungs ,

sections.label: Section 1055
sections.original: ῥοφεῖ ξυνοικοῦν , ἐκ δὲ χλωρὸν αἷμά μου
sections.translation: making my body its home . Already it has drunk away my fresh lifeblood , and my whole body is wasted , conquered by these indescribable bonds . Not spearmen on the battlefield , nor the Giants ’ earth-born army , nor the might of savage beasts ,

sections.label: Section 1060
sections.original: οὔθʼ Ἑλλὰς οὔτʼ ἄγλωσσος οὔθʼ ὅσην ἐγὼ
sections.translation:
not Hellas , nor the land of the barbarian , nor any land which I came to purify has ever done this to me . No , a woman , a weak woman , born not to the strength of man , all alone has brought me down without a stroke of the sword !
Son , show yourself my trueborn son ,

sections.label: Section 1065
sections.original: καὶ μὴ τὸ μητρὸς ὄνομα πρεσβεύσῃς πλέον .
sections.translation: and do not honor your mother ’ s name above your father ’ s . Bring out the woman that bore you , and give her with your own hands into my hand , that I may know for certain which sight grieves you more — my tortured frame , or hers , when she suffers her just punishment !

sections.label: Section 1070
sections.original: ἴθʼ , ὦ τέκνον , τόλμησον · οἴκτιρόν τέ με
sections.translation: Go , my son , be bold ! Show your pity for me , whom many might think deserving of pity — pity me moaning and weeping like a girl ! No one could say that he had ever seen this man do that before . No , always without complaint I used to pursue my troubles .

sections.label: Section 1075
sections.original: νῦν δʼ ἐκ τοιούτου θῆλυς ηὕρημαι τάλας .
sections.translation: But now in my misery I have been found a woman , instead of the man I used to be .

sections.label: Section 1076
sections.original: καὶ νῦν προσελθὼν στῆθι πλησίον πατρός ,
sections.translation: Come close , stand near your father and do examine the magnitude of the misfortune by which I suffer ; for I will uncover my suffering . Look ! See all of you this miserable body ;

sections.label: Section 1080
sections.original: ὁρᾶτε τὸν δύστηνον , ὡς οἰκτρῶς ἔχω .
sections.translation:
see how wretched , how pitiable I am !
Ah , misery ! The ruinous spasm flames again ; it shoots through my sides — I must wrestle once more with that cruel , devouring plague !

sections.label: Section 1085
sections.original: ὦναξ Ἀίδη , δέξαι μʼ ,
sections.translation: King Hades , receive me ! Strike me , O fire of Zeus ! Hurl down your thunderbolt , ruler , dash it , Father , upon my head ! Again the pest consumes me , it has blazed up , it has leapt to fury ! O hands , my hands ,

sections.label: Section 1090
sections.original: ὦ νῶτα καὶ στέρνʼ , ὦ φίλοι βραχίονες ,
sections.translation: O shoulders and chest and trusty arms , you are indeed those noted arms which once subdued with your might the dweller in Nemea , the scourge of herdsmen , the lion , a creature that no man might approach or confront ; you tamed the Lernaean Hydra ,

sections.label: Section 1095
sections.original: διφυῆ τʼ ἄμικτον ἱπποβάμονα στρατὸν
sections.translation: and that monstrous army of beasts with double form , hostile , going on hoofed feet , violent , lawless , of surpassing violence ; you tamed the beast in Erymanthia , and underground the three-headed whelp of Hades , a resistless terror , offspring of the fierce Echidna ; you tamed the dragon

sections.label: Section 1100
sections.original: δράκοντα μήλων φύλακʼ ἐπʼ ἐσχάτοις τόποις .
sections.translation: that guarded the golden fruit in the farthest places of the earth . These toils and thousands more have I tasted , and no man has ever erected a trophy of victory over my hands . But now , with joints unhinged and with flesh torn to shreds , I have become the miserable spoil of an unseen destroyer ,

sections.label: Section 1105
sections.original: ὁ τῆς ἀρίστης μητρὸς ὠνομασμένος ,
sections.translation:
— I , who am called the son of noblest mother , I , who am reputed the seed of Zeus , lord of the starry sky .
But you may be sure of one thing : though I am nothing , though I cannot move a step , yet she who has done this deed shall feel my heavy hand even so . Let her but come to me

sections.label: Section 1110
sections.original: ἵνʼ ἐκδιδαχθῇ πᾶσιν ἀγγέλλειν ὅτι
sections.translation: so that she may learn to proclaim this message to all the world , that in my death , as in my life , I punished the guilty !

sections.label: Section 1112
sections.original: ὦ τλῆμον Ἑλλάς , πένθος οἷον εἰσορῶ
sections.translation: Ah , unhappy Greece , what mourning do I foresee for her , if she is cheated of this man !

sections.label: Section 1114
sections.original: ἐπεὶ παρέσχες ἀντιφωνῆσαι , πάτερ ,
sections.translation: Father , since your pause permits an answer ,

sections.label: Section 1115
sections.original: σιγὴν παρασχὼν κλῦθί μου , νοσῶν ὅμως ·
sections.translation: hear me , diseased though you are ; I will ask you for no more than is my due . Give yourself to me in a mood not as harsh as that to which your heart is now stung . Otherwise you cannot learn in what circumstances you wrongly wish to triumph and wrongly show resentment .

sections.label: Section 1120
sections.original: εἰπὼν ὃ χρῄζεις λῆξον · ὡς ἐγὼ νοσῶν
sections.translation: Stop when you have said what it is you desire . In this pain of mine I understand none of your many riddles .

sections.label: Section 1122
sections.original: τῆς μητρὸς ἥκω τῆς ἐμῆς φράσων ἐν οἷς
sections.translation: I come to tell you of my mother — her present circumstances and how she erred unknowingly .

sections.label: Section 1124
sections.original: ὦ παγκάκιστε , καὶ παρεμνήσω γὰρ αὖ
sections.translation: You corrupt thing ! Have you indeed mentioned her name again ,

sections.label: Section 1125
sections.original: τῆς πατροφόντου μητρός , ὡς κλύειν ἐμέ ;
sections.translation: the name , “ Mother , Murderess of Father ,” in my hearing ?

sections.label: Section 1126
sections.original: ἔχει γὰρ οὕτως ὥστε μὴ σιγᾶν πρέπειν .
sections.translation: Yes , for her condition is such that my silence shames me .

sections.label: Section 1127
sections.original: οὐ δῆτα τοῖς γε πρόσθεν ἡμαρτημένοις .
sections.translation: No , it does not shame you , when you consider her past crimes .

sections.label: Section 1128
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐδὲ μὲν δὴ τοῖς γʼ ἐφʼ ἡμέραν ἐρεῖς .
sections.translation: You will not say so , at least in view of her deeds today .

sections.label: Section 1129
sections.original: λέγʼ , εὐλαβοῦ δὲ μὴ φανῇς κακὸς γεγώς .
sections.translation: Speak — but take care that you not be found corrupt .

sections.label: Section 1130
sections.original: λέγω · τέθνηκεν ἀρτίως νεοσφαγής .
sections.translation: I will speak . She is just now dead , a new murder .

sections.label: Section 1131
sections.original: πρὸς τοῦ ; τέρας τοι διὰ κακῶν ἐθέσπισας .
sections.translation: By whose hand ? Your sinister words give a prophecy of wonder !

sections.label: Section 1132
sections.original: αὐτὴ πρὸς αὑτῆς , οὐδενὸς πρὸς ἐκτόπου .
sections.translation: She did it by her own hand , and no other ’ s .

sections.label: Section 1133
sections.original: οἴμοι · πρὶν ὡς χρῆν σφʼ ἐξ ἐμῆς θανεῖν χερός ;
sections.translation: Ah , no ! Before she died by mine as she deserved !

sections.label: Section 1134
sections.original: κἂν σοῦ στραφείη θυμός , εἰ τὸ πᾶν μάθοις .
sections.translation: Even your rage would be deflected , if you would learn the whole of it .

sections.label: Section 1135
sections.original: δεινοῦ λόγου κατῆρξας · εἰπὲ δʼ ᾗ νοεῖς .
sections.translation: Your tale begins strangely , but tell me what you mean .

sections.label: Section 1136
sections.original: ἅπαν τὸ χρῆμʼ , ἥμαρτε χρηστὰ μωμένη .
sections.translation: The sum is this : she did wrong , but with a good intent .

sections.label: Section 1137
sections.original: χρήστʼ , ὦ κάκιστε , πατέρα σὸν κτείνασα δρᾷ ;
sections.translation: Did she do a good deed , corrupt traitor , when she killed your father ?

sections.label: Section 1138
sections.original: στέργημα γὰρ δοκοῦσα προσβαλεῖν σέθεν
sections.translation: No , her plan was to apply a love-charm for your heart , when she saw your new marriage inside the house , but she missed her aim .

sections.label: Section 1140
sections.original: καὶ τίς τοσοῦτος φαρμακεὺς Τραχινίων ;
sections.translation: And what Trachinian is so potent a charmer ?

sections.label: Section 1141
sections.original: Νέσσος πάλαι Κένταυρος ἐξέπεισέ νιν
sections.translation: Nessus the Centaur persuaded her long ago to inflame your desire with such a potion .

sections.label: Section 1143
sections.original: ἰοὺ ἰοὺ δύστηνος , οἴχομαι τάλας ·
sections.translation: Ah ! Ah , misery ! I am lost , ruined , ruined ! The light of day exists for me no more !

sections.label: Section 1145
sections.original: οἴμοι , φρονῶ δὴ ξυμφορᾶς ἵνʼ ἕσταμεν .
sections.translation: Ah , now I understand the depth of my misfortune ! Go , my son ! Your father is no more . Summon for me all the crop of your brothers . Summon , too , poor Alcmena , in vain the bedfellow of Zeus , so that

sections.label: Section 1150
sections.original: φήμην πύθησθε θεσφάτων ὅσʼ οἶδʼ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: you may hear my final words tell what oracles I know .

sections.label: Section 1151
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὔτε μήτηρ ἐνθάδʼ , ἀλλʼ ἐπακτίᾳ
sections.translation: I cannot . Your mother is not here . It happens that she keeps her home at Tiryns by the sea . Some of your children she has taken to raise with her there , and others , you will find , are dwelling in Thebes .

sections.label: Section 1155
sections.original: ἡμεῖς δʼ ὅσοι πάρεσμεν , εἴ τι χρή , πάτερ ,
sections.translation: But we who are with you , Father , will render at your command all service that is needed .

sections.label: Section 1157
sections.original: σὺ δʼ οὖν ἄκουε τοὔργον · ἐξήκεις δʼ ἵνα
sections.translation:
Hear , then , your task . You have come to where you will reveal what sort of man you are , who are called my son .
It was foreshown to me by my father far in the past that I would

sections.label: Section 1160
sections.original: τῶν ἐμπνεόντων μηδενὸς θανεῖν ὕπο ,
sections.translation: perish by no creature that had the breath of life , but by one already dead , a dweller with Hades . So this savage Centaur in death has killed me alive , just as the divine will had been foretold . And I will show you how

sections.label: Section 1165
sections.original: μαντεῖα καινά , τοῖς πάλαι ξυνήγορα ,
sections.translation: later oracles tally with the first and testify to the old prophecy . I wrote them down for myself from the mouth of my father ’ s oak of many tongues in the grove of the Selli , who dwell on the hills and sleep on the ground . The tree said that , at the time which lives and now is ,

sections.label: Section 1170
sections.original: ἔφασκε μόχθων τῶν ἐφεστώτων ἐμοὶ
sections.translation: my release from the toils laid upon me would be accomplished . And I expected prosperous days , but the meaning , it seems , was only that I would die . For toil comes no more to the dead . Since , then , my son , those words are clearly finding their fulfillment ,

sections.label: Section 1175
sections.original: δεῖ σʼ αὖ γενέσθαι τῷδε τἀνδρὶ σύμμαχον
sections.translation: you , on your part , must fight on my behalf . You must not delay , and so sharpen my tongue , but give your consent to work with me , like one who has discovered that best of laws , the law of obedience to your father .

sections.label: Section 1179
sections.original: ἀλλʼ , ὦ πάτερ , ταρβῶ μὲν εἰς λόγου στάσιν
sections.translation: Yes , Father — though in coming to this point in our talk

sections.label: Section 1180
sections.original: τοιάνδʼ ἐπελθών , πείσομαι δʼ ἅ σοι δοκεῖ .
sections.translation: I begin to have some fear — I will obey you in all your decrees .

sections.label: Section 1181
sections.original: ἔμβαλλε χεῖρα δεξιὰν πρώτιστά μοι ·
sections.translation: First of all , put your right hand in mine .

sections.label: Section 1182
sections.original: ὡς πρὸς τί πίστιν τήνδʼ ἄγαν ἐπιστρέφεις ;
sections.translation: With what purpose in mind do you so strongly urge this pledge on me ?

sections.label: Section 1183
sections.original: οὐ θᾶσσον οἴσεις μηδʼ ἀπιστήσεις ἐμοί ;
sections.translation: Give your hand at once — do not disobey me !

sections.label: Section 1184
sections.original: ἰδοὺ προτείνω , κοὐδὲν ἀντειρήσεται .
sections.translation: Here , I hold it out to you . Nothing will be denied you .

sections.label: Section 1185
sections.original: ὄμνυ Διός νυν τοῦ με φύσαντος κάρα ,
sections.translation: Now , swear by the head of Zeus my begetter !

sections.label: Section 1186
sections.original: ἦ μὴν τί δράσειν ; καὶ τόδʼ ἐξειρήσεται ;
sections.translation: To do what deed ? May this also be revealed ?

sections.label: Section 1187
sections.original: ἦ μὴν ἐμοὶ τὸ λεχθὲν ἔργον ἐκτελεῖν .
sections.translation: To perform for me the task that I shall impose .

sections.label: Section 1188
sections.original: ὄμνυμʼ ἔγωγε , Ζῆνʼ ἔχων ἐπώμοτον .
sections.translation: I swear it with Zeus for witness of the oath .

sections.label: Section 1189
sections.original: εἰ δʼ ἐκτὸς ἔλθοις , πημονὰς εὔχου λαβεῖν .
sections.translation: And pray that , if you break this oath , you may suffer .

sections.label: Section 1190
sections.original: οὐ μὴ λάβω · δράσω γάρ · εὔχομαι δʼ ὅμως ·
sections.translation: I shall not suffer , since I will keep it . Yet so I pray .
